title: Sunset Boat Trip in Marbella: Costs, Tips and Best Boats for 2026
html:
Marbella offers some of the Costa del Sol’s finest sunset vistas from the water. A typical evening cruise departs from Puerto Banús and costs from €70 per person for a shared tour. BoatHire24 connects you with local operators for a memorable experience.

Where to set off in Marbella

Puerto Banús is the most famous departure point, with its superyacht-lined dockside. Real Club Náutico de Marbella offers a more traditional marina in the town centre. Cabopino Marina, east of Marbella, provides access to the sand dunes of Artola. Marbella Bay stretches from the Golden Mile to Punta Romano. For a quieter start, the small fishing harbour at Puerto de la Bajadilla is ideal. Nearby, the island of Torreguadiaro is a frequent destination for sunset trips.

Which boat to rent

  1. Sunseeker Predator 72: A sleek motor yacht perfect for groups of up to 12; from €1,500 for a half-day sunset trip.
  2. Azimut 58: Offers spacious decks and cabin accommodation for 8 guests; from €1,200 for a three-hour evening charter.
  3. Lagoon 46 catamaran: Stable and roomy, ideal for families or 10 friends; from €1,800 for a full-day voyage.
  4. Axopar 28: A fast, open boat for 6 adventurers; from €400 for a two-hour sunset blast along the coast.
  5. Fairline Squadron 50: Classic British styling with luxury for 10 guests; from €1,600 for a half-day cruise.

For a closer look at the Sunseeker Predator 72, visit the Sunseeker Predator 72 listing on BoatHire24.

How much does a sunset boat trip cost in Marbella?

Prices vary from €70 per person for shared tours to over €2,000 for private crewed yachts. Here is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared sunset catamaran tour: €70, €90 per person for 2, 3 hours.
  • Small motorboat (e.g., Axopar) half-day: from €400 for up to 6 guests.
  • Catamaran full-day with crew: from €1,800 for up to 12 passengers.
  • Private crewed motor yacht (Sunseeker, Azimut) half-day: €1,200, €2,500.

BoatHire24 makes it easy to compare these options: start your search for a yacht charter Marbella cruise that fits your budget.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

In Spain, a licence is required for bareboat charters, but skippered vessels need none. For self-drive boats under 6 metres and up to 40 hp, a recreational licence issued in any EU country generally suffices. For larger hire boats, a skipper is mandatory and included in the price. Always verify exact requirements with the operator.

Book a skipper-included charter in Marbella: you get a local expert who knows the best anchorages for sunset, from Cabo Pino to the Marbella lighthouse.

When is the best season for a sunset boat trip?

High season runs from June to September, with peak demand in July and August. The prevailing Poniente wind (west) often calms by evening, creating a smooth sea. May and October also offer pleasant conditions with lighter crowds and lower prices. Nights are balmy, but bring a light jacket for the breeze.

At a glance

  • Price band: €70, €2,500 per trip
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2, 4 hours typical
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 on standard charters
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I bring my own food and drink on a sunset charter?

Most private charters allow you to bring your own provisions, and some include a welcome drink or tapas.

What is the typical sunset timing in Marbella?

Sunset occurs between 20:30 in June and 19:00 in October, with the golden hour lasting about 40 minutes.

Are sunset trips suitable for children?

Yes, catamarans and motor yachts are child-friendly; life jackets are provided for all ages.

How far do sunset cruises go?

Trips typically cover 5, 10 nautical miles along the coast, often passing Puerto Banús and the Marbella shoreline.
